steps.title: Use circle standard form
steps.content: A circle with center $(h,k)$ and radius $r$ has this form.

steps.title: Substitute the center and radius
steps.content:
The center is $(17,14)$ and the radius is $7k$, so:
$$
(x-17)^2+(y-14)^2=(7k)^2
$$

steps.title: Square the radius
steps.content: Square the radius term: $$ (7k)^2=49k^2 $$ So the equation is: $$ (x-17)^2+(y-14)^2=49k^2 $$ The answer is B.
